step_rationale: Remodels sphingomyelin and ceramides; dysregulation affects lipoprotein composition and atherogenicity.
mechanism: SGMS2 regulates ceramide and sphingomyelin metabolism, affecting lipoprotein atherogenicity and subendothelial lipid accumulation.

references.title: Lipidomics reveals the dysregulated ceramide metabolism in oxidized low-density lipoprotein-induced macrophage-derived foam cell.
